D4 Colorimetry L2 Understand Atom

The HSL Double Cone

slide the lightness and watch the colour disc widen to full chroma at the middle, then pinch to white

HSL splits brightness symmetrically: black at the bottom apex, white at the top, the most saturated colours around the equator. The solid is two cones base to base.

Slide the lightness. The disc's radius is the chroma available at that level — widest at L = 50%, shrinking to a point at pure black and pure white. The side profile shows the cut in the double cone.
